Skip to main content 💀 End the World Concentrating Wealth

Distributed wealth is inefficient. Capital allocation is demonstrably superior when managed by those who already understand capital — i.e., those who have the most of it. Progressive redistribution introduces friction, regulatory overhead, and moral hazard. The invisible hand works best when it's wearing a Patek Philippe.
$206 trillion

Net worth of the global top 1%. An efficient allocation, by any measure.
The Playbook Offshore Everything

The Cayman Islands are a feature, not a bug. International tax architecture exists to be optimized. Every dollar sheltered is a dollar protected from bureaucratic waste.
Gut Progressive Taxation

Frame tax cuts as "job creation" and "economic stimulus." The evidence that they primarily benefit the wealthy is irrelevant if the messaging is sufficiently compelling.
Predatory Lending

Underserved markets offer high margins and limited regulatory oversight. Payday lending, subprime mortgages, and high-interest microloans extract value efficiently from populations with few alternatives.
Strategic Union Busting

Organized labor compresses margins. Invest in anti-union consulting, right-to-work legislation, and gig economy reclassification. Frame it as "worker flexibility."
Privatize Public Goods

Water, education, healthcare, and transit are all undermonetized. Each represents a captive market with inelastic demand — the ideal conditions for rent extraction. Water is particularly promising.
Implementation Roadmap
  1. Engage wealth management firms with demonstrated offshore optimization expertise
  2. Fund think tanks that produce research supporting supply-side economics
  3. Identify public utility privatization opportunities in fiscally stressed municipalities
  4. Establish PAC infrastructure for pro-business legislative candidates
  5. Develop consumer financial products with appropriately complex fee structures
 Recommended Reading

"Regulatory Arbitrage: A Practitioner's Guide" — Alpine Capital Advisory

"The Case for Wealth Concentration: A First-Principles Analysis" — Heritage Analytics

"Extracting Value from the Base of the Pyramid" — Emerging Markets Quarterly
